item.title: Oil slides as US official says Hormuz transits are ‘meaningfully’ climbing
item.description: Energy secretary Chris Wright cautions that flows through the strait will take months to fully recover

item.title: Nasdaq sinks more than 3% as tech sell-off reignites
item.description: Heavy losses for AI-linked stocks overshadow gains across rest of market

item.title: GSK’s $10.6bn cancer deal will lead to more
item.description: Making additional moves would be in keeping with pharma group’s strategy of building its pipeline as fast as possible

item.title: Space strawberry grower grows share count before strawberries
item.description: Extract on space, repent at leisure

item.title: Pentagon restores Alibaba, Baidu and BYD to Chinese military groups blacklist
item.description: Three companies reinstated as US national security risk after sudden removal in February

item.title: Boots in talks over $10bn sale as owners look to ditch IPO plan
item.description: Weston family and Australian group Sigma Healthcare both interested in buying UK pharmacy chain

item.title: Brussels orders Meta to open WhatsApp to rival AI agents
item.description: Emergency measures signal EU efforts to intervene over rapidly developing market for autonomous agents

item.title: VodafoneThree bids for TalkTalk consumer business
item.description: Acquisition would help UK’s largest mobile operator fulfil ambition to double its broadband business

item.title: Urban pollution in wealthy world still adding to heart damage, study finds
item.description: Canadian research linking CT scans to pollutants shows severity of cardiovascular effects

item.title: BP investors push for change at ‘mismanaged kingdom’
item.description: Lack of detail on why Albert Manifold was fired sows doubts over culture overhaul

item.title: How to Rule the World — Stanford’s lucrative bond with Silicon Valley
item.description: The elite California university produces an outsized share of start-up entrepreneurs — and outsized profits from its proximity to Big Tech

item.title: Woodford’s W4.0 hits out at FCA’s legal threat
item.description: Regulator said it would seek to injunct stockpicker’s new firm over alleged unauthorised advice

item.title: Real Madrid and the dangers of mixing fans and finance
item.description: Club president Florentino Pérez’s aim of bringing in outside investors is high risk

item.title: How Poland’s Orlen kept supplies flowing during the Middle East war
item.description: Group’s head oil trader said the company learned lessons from the 2022 shock

item.title: ‘Rent now, pay later’ loans target US consumers squeezed by housing costs
item.description: Demand for short-term financing expands into rental market amid deepening affordability crisis

item.title: PwC investigated by accounting regulator over WHSmith audit
item.description: FRC to probe Big Four firm’s work after UK travel retailer revealed accounting errors in US business

item.title: So long Tate & Lyle, stock market trivia titan
item.description: Sweetest goodbye or sticky situation?

item.title: Oil market defies predictions of summer supply crunch
item.description: Crude prices stay below $100 a barrel as China’s import cuts help eke out global stockpiles

item.title: GSK to buy US cancer biotech Nuvalent for $10.6bn
item.description: New chief Luke Miels makes his mark at GSK with one of UK drugmakers’ biggest deals

item.title: Airbus plans German-led alliance to replace doomed fighter jet
item.description: Group of eight companies want to develop a sixth-generation fighter jet for Europe

item.title: AI offers European drug gangs a tech boost
item.description: EU agency chief warns traffickers are tapping new technologies to develop and deliver illegal substances

item.title: Dutch supercar brand Spyker wins crypto backing in latest revival bid
item.description: Ukrainian billionaire Volodymyr Nosov acquires ‘significant’ stake in carmaker

item.title: Asian tech stocks rebound after Wall Street embraces AI once more
item.description: Chipmaker-dominant markets rally after sell-off on Monday

item.title: European satellite group Iceye in €1bn funding led by General Atlantic
item.description: Capital raise values Finnish-Polish group at €10bn

item.title: We’ve died and gone to volatility heaven
item.description: Plus non-dollar stablecoins

item.title: What Aristotle can teach us about AI-enabled quantitative investment
item.description: Unexpectedly, according to one broker, the answer is not nothing

item.title: FirstFT: SpaceX investors asked to buy Musk’s ‘moonshots’
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Hungary’s anti-graft AI and GSK-Nuvalent merger talks

item.title: Africa’s Fastest-Growing Companies
item.description: South Africa dominates the 2026 rankings as Kenya leapfrogs Nigeria into second place and an Egyptian business tops the list for the first time. Plus: fintechs offer a fix to Africa’s financial problems and a solar success with more than 50mn customers across the continent

item.title: Inside the battle to break up the world’s oldest bank
item.description: Monte dei Paschi emerged from public ownership to become an improbable consolidator. Intesa and BPM have it in their sights

item.title: Italy’s World Cup of banking drama
item.description: Rival moves for Monte dei Paschi are setting the stage for a rare takeover battle in Italian banking
